member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.DeploymentItemAttribute
member.summary: Used to specify a deployment item (file or directory) for per-test deployment. The specified files and folders are copied to the .DeploymentDirectory, which is the directory from which the test assembly is executed and where all deployment items are present alongside the test source DLL. Can be specified on a test class or a test method. Can have multiple instances of the attribute to specify more than one item. The item path can be absolute or relative; if relative, it's resolved against the build output directory (the folder that contains the test assembly).
member.remarks: If specified on a test class, the class needs to contain at least one test method. This means that the attribute cannot be combined with a test class that would contain only an AssemblyInitialize or ClassInitialize method.

example.code: [DeploymentItem("file1.xml")] // Copy file1.xml from the build output directory to the deployment directory. [DeploymentItem("Resources/file2.xml", "DataFiles")] // Copy file2.xml from the Resources subfolder into a DataFiles subfolder of the deployment directory. [DeploymentItem("TestFiles/")] // Copy all files and subfolders of the TestFiles folder to the deployment directory.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.DeploymentItemAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The file or directory to deploy. The path is relative to the build output directory. The item will be copied to the same directory as the deployed test assemblies.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.DeploymentItemAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The relative or absolute path to the file or directory to deploy. The path is relative to the build output directory. The item will be copied to the same directory as the deployed test assemblies.

The path of the directory to which the items are to be copied. It can be either absolute or relative to the deployment directory. All files and directories identified by will be copied to this directory.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.DeploymentItemAttribute.Path
member.summary: Gets the path of the source file or folder to be copied.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.DeploymentItemAttribute.OutputDirectory
member.summary: Gets the path of the directory to which the item is copied.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.ITestRunInfo
member.summary: Read-only ambient information about the current test run. Accessed via .
member.remarks: Unlike , which describes the test currently executing, an describes the run as a whole and is queryable from any code reachable during a test run (for example helpers, fixtures, or [AssemblyInitialize] methods).

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.ITestRunInfo.PlannedTests
member.summary: Gets the tests that have been discovered and have passed the active filter for the currently-executing assembly. The collection is empty until the platform begins executing tests for an assembly.
member.remarks: Data-driven tests whose rows are unfolded only at execution time may appear here as a single entry rather than one entry per row.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.MessageLevel
member.summary: Specifies the severity level of messages displayed using the API.

member.name: F:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.MessageLevel.Informational
member.summary: The message will be displayed as informational, typically used for general updates or non-critical messages.

member.name: F:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.MessageLevel.Warning
member.summary: The message will be displayed as a warning, indicating a potential issue or something requiring attention.

member.name: F:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.MessageLevel.Error
member.summary: The message will be displayed as an error, representing a significant issue or failure.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.PlannedTest
member.summary: Represents a single test that is planned to run (i.e. has been discovered and passed the active filter) in the current test run. Returned by .
member.remarks: Instances are immutable snapshots produced at the start of test execution for the assembly. They are intended for read-only inspection (for example to decide whether expensive setup is needed in [AssemblyInitialize]); they do not carry execution-time state such as outcome, exceptions, or data-driven rows.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The fully-qualified name of the class declaring the test method.
The simple name of the test method.
The display name of the test, when set; otherwise .
The full path of the assembly that declares the test, on disk.
The ECMA-335 managed type name for the declaring type, when available.
The ECMA-335 managed method name (encoding parameter types) for the test method, when available.
The source file that declares the test, captured at compile-time by , when available.
The line number within at which the test is declared, when available.
The categories applied via .
The name/value pairs applied via . Multiple entries with the same name are preserved.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.PlannedTest.CreateFromOwnedArrays(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.String[],System.Collections.Generic.KeyValuePair{System.String,System.String}[])
member.summary: Internal factory used by the adapter to construct a without the defensive copy performed by the public constructor. The supplied arrays MUST NOT be retained or mutated by the caller after this call.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.PlannedTest.FullyQualifiedTestClassName
member.summary: Gets the fully-qualified name of the class declaring the test method. Mirrors .

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.PlannedTest.TestName
member.summary: Gets the simple name of the test method. Mirrors .

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.PlannedTest.TestDisplayName
member.summary: Gets the display name of the test, when one was set; otherwise . Mirrors .

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.PlannedTest.AssemblyPath
member.summary: Gets the full path of the assembly that declares the test, on disk.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.PlannedTest.ManagedTypeName
member.summary: Gets the ECMA-335 managed type name for the declaring type, when available.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.PlannedTest.ManagedMethodName
member.summary: Gets the ECMA-335 managed method name (encoding parameter types) for the test method, when available. Use this for unambiguous identification of overloaded methods.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.PlannedTest.DeclaringFilePath
member.summary: Gets the source file path that declares the test. This value is captured at compile time by ; it can be when the test is declared via a custom test-method attribute that does not propagate the caller information.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.PlannedTest.DeclaringLineNumber
member.summary: Gets the line number within at which the test is declared, when available; otherwise .

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.PlannedTest.TestCategories
member.summary: Gets the categories applied to this test via at the method, class, or assembly level.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.PlannedTest.TestProperties
member.summary: Gets the name/value pairs applied to this test via . Multiple attributes with the same name are preserved as separate entries.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestContext
member.summary: Used to store information that is provided to unit tests.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestContext.Current
member.summary: Gets the current instance.
member.remarks: This property returns the context for the currently executing test. When accessed outside of a test execution, it returns .

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestContext.Properties
member.summary: Gets test properties for a test.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestContext.CancellationTokenSource
member.summary: Gets or sets the cancellation token source. This token source is canceled when test times out. Also when explicitly canceled the test will be aborted.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestContext.CancellationToken
member.summary: Gets the cancellation token. This token is canceled when test times out. Also when explicitly canceled the test will be aborted.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestContext.TestData
member.summary: Gets or sets the test data for the test method being executed.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestContext.TestDisplayName
member.summary: Gets or sets the test display name for the test method being executed.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestContext.TestRunDirectory
member.summary: Gets the top-level directory for the test run, under which deployed files and result files are stored.

remarks.para: This is the root of the layout used by the other deployment directory properties. A typical run produces:
remarks.code: <solution>\TestResults\<run-guid> // TestRunDirectory \In // ResultsDirectory and TestResultsDirectory \<MachineName> // TestRunResultsDirectory \Out // DeploymentDirectory

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestContext.DeploymentDirectory
member.summary: Gets the directory for files deployed for the test run (the "Out" directory). This is typically the Out subdirectory of .
member.remarks: When app domains are disabled, this can instead point at the test assembly directory.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestContext.ResultsDirectory
member.summary: Gets the base directory for results from the test run (the "In" directory). This is typically the In subdirectory of .
member.remarks: In the current implementation this returns the same path as (the In directory). When run directory information is unavailable, the platform services layer can fall back to the application base directory.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestContext.TestRunResultsDirectory
member.summary: Gets the per-machine directory for test run result files. This is the <MachineName> subdirectory of (i.e. In\<MachineName>).

remarks.para: Despite the similar names, this directory is typically a child of , not the other way around. For example, with set to ...\In on a machine named BUILD01:
When run directory information is unavailable, the platform services layer can return the same path for both properties.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestContext.TestResultsDirectory
member.summary: Gets the directory for test result files (the "In" directory). This is the same path as and is typically the parent of . When run directory information is unavailable, the platform services layer can return the same path for both properties.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestContext.FullyQualifiedTestClassName
member.summary: Gets the Fully-qualified name of the class containing the test method currently being executed.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestContext.TestName
member.summary: Gets the name of the test method currently being executed.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestContext.CurrentTestOutcome
member.summary: Gets the current test outcome.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestContext.TestException
member.summary: Gets or sets the exception that occurred in the TestInitialize or TestMethod steps.
member.remarks: The property is always null when accessed during the TestInitialize or TestMethod steps.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestContext.TestRunCount
member.summary: Gets the current attempt of the test run. This property is relevant when using (or any implementation of ). On the first run, this property is set to 1. On subsequent retries, the value is incremented.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestContext.AddResultFile(System.String)
member.summary: Adds a file name to the list in TestResult.ResultFileNames.
member.param: The file Name.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestContext.Write(System.String)
member.summary: Used to write trace messages while the test is running.
member.param: formatted message string.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestContext.Write(System.String,System.Object[])
member.summary: Used to write trace messages while the test is running.
member.param: format string.
the arguments.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestContext.WriteLine(System.String)
member.summary: Used to write trace messages while the test is running.
member.param: formatted message string.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestContext.WriteLine(System.String,System.Object[])
member.summary: Used to write trace messages while the test is running.
member.param: format string.
the arguments.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestContext.DisplayMessage(Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.MessageLevel,System.String)
member.summary: Used to write trace messages while the test is running.
member.param: The message level.
The message.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestRun
member.summary: Provides ambient access to information about the currently-executing test run.

remarks.para: always returns a non- ; before tests start executing it exposes empty collections.
The information is scoped to the current process and (on .NET Framework) the current AppDomain. Cross-process or cross-AppDomain test runs each have their own snapshot.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestRun.Current
member.summary: Gets information about the currently-executing test run. Never returns .

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestRun.SetCurrent(Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.ITestRunInfo)
member.summary: Set by the platform before executing tests for an assembly. Pass to reset to the empty implementation.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.Resources.FrameworkExtensionsMessages.ArgumentXMustDeriveFromClassY
member.summary: Type '{0}' is not assignable to '{1}'.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.Resources.FrameworkExtensionsMessages.AsyncUITestMethodWithNoDispatcherQueue
member.summary: UITestMethodAttribute.DispatcherQueue should not be null. To use UITestMethodAttribute within a WinUI Desktop App, remember to set the static UITestMethodAttribute.DispatcherQueue during the test initialization.
